9	# Feature: DDx Installation Architecture
10	
11	**ID:** FEAT-015
12	**Status:** In Progress
13	**Priority:** P0
14	**Owner:** DDx Team
15	
16	> **Update 2026-04-29 (project-local install, no symlinks):** This feature
17	> is amended to drop the global skill installation model entirely. DDx no
18	> longer installs skills under `~/.agents/skills/` or `~/.claude/skills/`,
19	> and `ddx install --global` is removed. `ddx init` and `ddx install
20	> <plugin>` only touch the current `<projectRoot>`. The new project-local
21	> tree (`<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`,
22	> `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`) is committed to git as real files.
23	> **Zero symlinks are created by ddx install** — see the new
24	> "Cross-platform invariant" section below for the Windows-compatibility
25	> reasoning and how plugin-author symlinks are handled. Existing projects
26	> with home-directory symlinks or stale `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries
27	> are handled per the new "Migration" section. Plugin manifests whose
28	> `Root.Target` begins with `~` are rejected with a non-zero exit.
29	> Sections, bullets, and acceptance criteria below that describe
30	> `ddx install --global`, `~/.ddx/skills/`, `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`,
31	> or `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` should be read as historical context;
32	> the amendment in this header and the two new sections are
33	> authoritative.
34	>
35	> **Update 2026-04-17:** The skill roster referenced throughout this
36	> document (ddx-bead, ddx-agent, ddx-install, ddx-status, ddx-review,
37	> ddx-run, ddx-doctor) reflects the pre-consolidation layout. Per
38	> FEAT-011, DDx now ships a **single portable `ddx` skill** with
39	> `SKILL.md` + `reference/*.md` (progressive disclosure, agentskills.io
40	> standard). The installation flow described below is still accurate
41	> in structure — binary separate from library, `ddx init` copies skills
42	> as real files, `ddx install <plugin>` adds plugin-scoped content —
43	> but any reference to the 7-skill roster should be read as historical
44	> context. Sections that specifically describe the bootstrap
45	> allowlist are now ["ddx"] instead of ["ddx-doctor", "ddx-run"], and
46	> the stale-skill cleanup removes all old ddx-prefixed dirs (ddx-bead,
47	> ddx-run, ddx-agent, ddx-review, ddx-status, ddx-doctor, ddx-install,
48	> ddx-release) on init and update. See FEAT-011 for the current skill
49	> architecture.
50	
51	## Overview
52	
53	Redesign the DDx installation architecture with a clean separation of concerns:
54	- **install.sh** — binary only (minimal attack surface, fast)
55	- **ddx init** — project-local: writes `<projectRoot>/.ddx/`,
56	  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`
57	  as real files (copied, never symlinked). Touches no path outside the
58	  project root.
59	- **ddx install \<plugin\>** — project-local: plugin tarball extracted
60	  into `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`, then plugin skills copied
61	  as real files into `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and
62	  `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. Touches no path outside the project
63	  root. Creates zero symlinks (see "Cross-platform invariant").
64	- All project-local install outputs (`<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`,
65	  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`)
66	  are intended to be committed to git so teammates and CI get the
67	  same skills on clone without re-running `ddx install`.
68	
69	## Cross-platform invariant
70	
71	**`ddx install` (and `ddx init`) creates zero symlinks.** Every file
72	written is a real file copied from the binary's embedded tree or from
73	an extracted plugin tarball.
74	
75	**Reasoning — Windows compatibility.** Symlinks on Windows require
76	either Developer Mode, an elevated process, or specific filesystem
77	features, and behave inconsistently across NTFS, ReFS, and shared
78	filesystems. A single project-local layout that works identically on
79	Linux, macOS, and Windows is worth the duplication cost of copied
80	files.
81	
82	**Plugin-source symlinks are not materialized.** If a plugin tarball
83	contains symlinks (e.g. an author symlinks `.claude/skills/foo` →
84	`../.agents/skills/foo` inside their source tree), DDx does **not**
85	materialize those symlinks on extract. Plugin authors are responsible
86	for shipping each skill as a real directory in every target the
87	plugin declares. A plugin that relies on tarball symlinks resolving
88	on the install side is a malformed plugin.
89	
90	**Manifest validation.** Plugin manifests are scanned at install
91	time. Any `Root.Target` (or equivalent install target) whose path
92	begins with `~` is rejected and `ddx install` exits with a non-zero
93	status. Targets must be project-relative paths under
94	`<projectRoot>/`. This invariant is enforced even for plugins that
95	worked under the pre-amendment global model.
96	
97	## Migration
98	
99	Pre-amendment installs may have left state in three places. This
100	section describes how DDx heals or ignores each.
101	
102	- **Home-directory symlinked skills** (`~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`,
103	  `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` pointing into `~/.ddx/skills/` or a project
104	  tree). `ddx update --force` (and `ddx init --force` inside an
105	  existing project) heals the project by rewriting
106	  `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/` and `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`
107	  as real files. DDx does **not** delete files outside the project
108	  root; users may `rm -rf ~/.agents/skills/ddx-*
109	  ~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` themselves if they want a clean home tree.
110	
111	- **Stale `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries** from a prior global
112	  install. These are treated as **no-op uninstalls**: `ddx
113	  uninstall <name>` succeeds without touching the filesystem when
114	  the recorded paths are outside the current project root, and the
115	  entry is removed from `installed.yaml`. No error is raised for
116	  missing files.
117	
118	- **`~/.ddx/plugins/`** from a prior global install. DDx leaves this
119	  directory in place as inert state — nothing reads from it under
120	  the project-local model. Users may `rm -rf ~/.ddx/plugins/` at
121	  their convenience; DDx will not recreate it.
122	
123	## Problem Statement
124	
125	Current behavior:
126	- `install.sh` does too much (creates `~/.ddx/`, sets up symlinks)
127	- `ddx install helix` clones to user home (`~/.ddx/plugins/`), not project-scoped
128	- Symlinks aren't tracked by git, so project-local `.agents/skills → .ddx/skills` breaks on clone
129	- No separation between global installation and project-scoped plugin management
130	
131	Desired behavior:
132	- `install.sh` does one thing: get the binary into PATH
133	- `ddx install --global` owns global skill setup (home directory)
134	- `ddx init` copies bootstrap skills as real files (git-trackable)
135	- `ddx install <plugin>` is project-scoped, uses relative symlinks for `.agents/` and `.claude/`
136	
137	## Requirements
138	
139	### Functional
140	
141	#### install.sh (curl script)
142	
143	1. **Binary-Only Installation**
144	   - Downloads `ddx` binary to `~/.local/bin/ddx`
145	   - Sets up PATH in shell rc file
146	   - Sets up shell completions
147	   - Does NOT create `~/.ddx/`, `~/.agents/`, or `~/.claude/`
148	
149	#### Global Installation (`ddx install --global`)
150	
151	2. **DDx Skills Extraction**
152	   - Extracts embedded skills (ddx-bead, ddx-agent, ddx-install, ddx-status, ddx-review, ddx-run) to `~/.ddx/skills/`
153	   - Creates `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*` symlinks → `~/.ddx/skills/ddx-*`
154	   - Creates `~/.claude/skills/ddx-*` symlinks → `~/.agents/skills/ddx-*`
155	
156	#### Repository Initialization (`ddx init`)
157	
158	3. **Project Structure Creation**
159	   - Creates `.ddx/` directory with config.yaml and library structure
160	   - Copies bootstrap skills (ddx-doctor, ddx-run) as **real files** to `.ddx/skills/`
161	   - Copies bootstrap skills to `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` as **real files**
162	   - All files are git-trackable (no symlinks for project-local skills)
163	
164	3a. **Bootstrap Skill Cleanup (Stale ddx-* Removal)**
165	   - Before copying bootstrap skills, scans each target directory (`.ddx/skills/`, `.agents/skills/`, `.claude/skills/`) for existing `ddx-*` subdirectories
166	   - Any `ddx-*` directory containing a `SKILL.md` that is **not** in the current bootstrap allowlist (`ddx-doctor`, `ddx-run`) is removed
167	   - Purpose: removes skills from older DDx versions that are no longer part of the bootstrap set
168	   - Only removes `ddx-*` prefixed directories; plugin skills (e.g., `helix-*`) are never touched
169	   - Silent: no user-visible output on cleanup; errors are ignored (non-fatal)
170	   - Runs on every `ddx init` invocation, including `ddx init --force`
171	
172	4. **Pre-flight Check**
173	   - Verify `ddx` binary exists in PATH
174	   - If missing: warn user, suggest running install.sh
175	
176	#### Plugin Installation (`ddx install <plugin>`)
177	
178	5. **Project-Scoped Plugin Install**
179	   - Default: downloads released tarball from plugin's GitHub releases
180	   - Extracts to `$PROJECT/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
181	   - Creates relative symlinks from `.agents/skills/<skill>` → `.ddx/plugins/<name>/.agents/skills/<skill>`
182	   - Creates relative symlinks from `.claude/skills/<skill>` → `.agents/skills/<skill>`
183	   - Relative symlinks work across clone/checkout (no absolute paths)
184	   - Fallback: `ddx install <plugin> --from-source` clones repo (for developers working on the plugin)
185	
186	5a. **Plugin Skill Stale Link Pruning**
187	   - Before creating new skill symlinks, scans the target skills directory for existing entries not in the plugin's current skill list
188	   - Removes a symlink only if **both** conditions hold: (a) it is a symlink (not a real file), and (b) its resolved target is within the plugin's installed root (`.ddx/plugins/<name>/`)
189	   - Purpose: removes symlinks for skills that were removed in a plugin update
190	   - Real files (e.g., bootstrap skills copied by `ddx init`) are never removed by this step
191	   - Symlinks from other plugins (pointing outside this plugin's root) are never removed
192	   - Runs during every `ddx install <plugin>` invocation
193	
194	5b. **Stale Install File Removal**
195	   - The plugin registry tracks the set of files written by each install
196	   - On re-install (update), files from the previous install that are absent from the new install's file list are removed
197	   - Applies to any file tracked in the plugin registry entry (e.g., extracted tarball contents)
198	   - Runs during `ddx install <plugin>` when a prior install record exists
199	
200	6. **Plugin Manifest**
201	   - Records installed plugins in `.ddx/plugins.yaml` or similar
202	   - Tracks name, version, install source (release vs source), install date
203	   - Enables `ddx installed` to show project-scoped plugins
204	   - Enables `ddx outdated` to check for newer released versions
205	
206	#### Version Tracking & Staleness Detection
207	
208	9. **Project Version Stamp (`.ddx/versions.yaml`)**
209	   - System-managed file, separate from user config. Users never edit.
210	   - Written by `ddx init`, committed to git alongside config.yaml
211	   - Contains single field: `ddx_version` — the binary version that initialized/last updated the project
212	   - Schema:
213	     ```yaml
214	     ddx_version: "0.3.0"
215	     ```
216	
217	10. **Version Gate (pre-run, every command)**
218	    - If `.ddx/versions.yaml` does not exist → skip (not a DDx project, or pre-versioning)
219	    - If binary version is `"dev"` → skip (development builds bypass gate)
220	    - If binary version < project's `ddx_version` → **hard error, block execution:**
221	      ```
222	      Error: This project requires DDx v0.4.0 or newer (you have v0.3.0).
223	      Run 'ddx upgrade' to update your DDx binary.
224	      ```
225	    - Exempt commands: `upgrade`, `version`, `doctor`, `init` (must work even when binary is too old)
226	    - Runs in `PersistentPreRunE`, after config init, before update check
227	    - Pure string compare — no network, no disk beyond the config read
228	
229	11. **Staleness Hints (post-run, every command)**
230	    - If binary version > project's `ddx_version` → soft hint:
231	      ```
232	      💡 Project skills from DDx v0.3.0 (you have v0.4.0). Run 'ddx init --force' to update.
233	      ```
234	    - Plugin staleness: compare `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` entries vs `BuiltinRegistry()` → soft hint:
235	      ```
236	      💡 helix 1.0.0 installed, 2.0.0 available. Run 'ddx install helix' to update.
237	      ```
238	    - Runs in `PersistentPostRunE`, after existing update-available banner
239	    - Pure local comparisons — no network
240	
241	12. **Force Refresh (`ddx init --force`)**
242	    - Overwrites `.ddx/versions.yaml` with current binary version
243	    - Overwrites existing skill files (currently `registerProjectSkills` skips existing files — must add `force` parameter)
244	    - Preserves user config in `.ddx/config.yaml`
245	
246	#### Updates
247	
248	7. **Binary Update (`ddx upgrade`)**
249	   - Checks GitHub for new ddx release (async, 24h cache via `~/.cache/ddx/last-update-check.json`)
250	   - Uses GitHub `releases/latest`, so prereleases do not trigger normal update detection unless intentionally promoted as the latest release
251	   - Version comparison must treat any hyphenated prerelease suffix as older than the matching stable release, with channel ordering `alpha < beta < rc < stable` and numeric ordering within the same channel (`rc2 < rc10`)
252	   - Downloads and replaces binary
253	   - After upgrade, next command in project shows staleness hint (correct: new binary > old `ddx_version`)
254	   - Dogfood installs of prereleases remain possible via explicit version selection, e.g. `DDX_VERSION=v0.3.0-rc1 curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/main/install.sh | bash`
255	
256	8. **Plugin Update (`ddx update <plugin>`)**
257	   - Checks plugin's GitHub releases for newer version
258	   - Downloads new release tarball to `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
259	   - Re-establishes relative symlinks
260	   - `ddx update <plugin> --from-source` re-clones from repo HEAD
261	
262	### Non-Functional
263	
264	- **No Repo Bloat:** plugins live in `.ddx/plugins/` (gitignored or committed per user preference)
265	- **Git-Trackable Skills:** `ddx init` copies real files, not symlinks
266	- **Git-Trackable Versions:** `.ddx/versions.yaml` committed to git — teammates get version gate on clone
267	- **Git-Trackable Execution Evidence:** `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` is
268	  the tracked execute-bead attempt bundle defined in FEAT-006 §"Execute-Bead
269	  Evidence Bundle". `ddx init` and any DDx-managed `.gitignore` template
270	  must leave `.ddx/executions/` trackable so bundles committed by
271	  `execute-bead` survive clones. Only the ignored runtime scratch paths
272	  listed in FEAT-006 (`.ddx/exec-runs.d/`, `.ddx/agent-logs/`,
273	  `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*/`) may be excluded from tracking.
274	- **Relative Symlinks for Plugins:** work across machines, no absolute paths
275	- **No Windows Targets:** relative symlinks are acceptable
276	- **Offline-First:** bootstrap skills work without network; version gate is local-only
277	- **Idempotent:** multiple runs of same command produce same result
278	- **Separation of Concerns:** `.ddx/config.yaml` for user preferences, `.ddx/versions.yaml` for system-managed state, `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` for global plugin state
279	
280	## Architecture
281	
282	### Directory Structure
283	
284	```
285	# Global (via ddx install --global)
286	~/.ddx/
287	├── skills/
288	│   ├── ddx-bead/
289	│   ├── ddx-agent/
290	│   ├── ddx-install/
291	│   ├── ddx-status/
292	│   ├── ddx-review/
293	│   └── ddx-run/
294	└── config.yaml
295	
296	~/.agents/skills/
297	├── ddx-bead/ → ~/.ddx/skills/ddx-bead/
298	├── ddx-agent/ → ~/.ddx/skills/ddx-agent/
299	└── ...
300	
301	~/.claude/skills/
302	├── ddx-bead/ → ~/.agents/skills/ddx-bead/
303	└── ...
304	
305	# Project (via ddx init + ddx install helix)
306	project/
307	├── .ddx/
308	│   ├── config.yaml       (user preferences)
309	│   ├── versions.yaml     (system-managed: ddx_version)
310	│   ├── library/
311	│   ├── skills/
312	│   │   ├── ddx-doctor/   (real files, git-tracked)
313	│   │   └── ddx-run/      (real files, git-tracked)
314	│   ├── executions/       (tracked execute-bead attempt bundles; see FEAT-006)
315	│   │   └── <attempt-id>/ (prompt.md, manifest.json, result.json, ...)
316	│   └── plugins/
317	│       └── helix/        (cloned plugin)
318	│           └── .agents/skills/
319	│               ├── helix-align/
320	│               ├── helix-build/
321	│               └── ...
322	├── .agents/skills/
323	│   ├── ddx-doctor/       (real files, copied by ddx init)
324	│   ├── ddx-run/          (real files, copied by ddx init)
325	│   ├── helix-align/ → ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align
326	│   ├── helix-build/ → ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-build
327	│   └── ...
328	├── .claude/skills/
329	│   ├── ddx-doctor/ → ../.agents/skills/ddx-doctor
330	│   ├── helix-align/ → ../.agents/skills/helix-align
331	│   └── ...
332	└── ...
333	```
334	
335	### Command Matrix
336	
337	| Command | Scope | What It Does |
338	|---------|-------|--------------|
339	| `curl install.sh \| bash` | Global | Binary to `~/.local/bin/ddx` + PATH |
340	| `ddx install --global` | Global | Extract skills to `~/.ddx/`, symlink `~/.agents/`, `~/.claude/` |
341	| `ddx init` | Project | `.ddx/` structure + copy bootstrap skills to `.agents/`, `.claude/` |
342	| `ddx install helix` | Project | Clone to `.ddx/plugins/helix/`, relative symlinks in `.agents/`, `.claude/` |
343	| `ddx upgrade` | Global | Update binary |
344	| `ddx update <plugin>` | Project | Re-clone plugin, re-establish symlinks |
345	
346	### Key Design Decisions
347	
348	1. **Copy over symlink for ddx init**: Git doesn't track symlinks well. Bootstrap skills must survive `git clone` on a fresh machine.
349	
350	2. **Relative symlinks for plugins**: Plugin skills are installed via relative symlinks (e.g., `../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align`). This works across machines without absolute paths. Acceptable since we're not targeting Windows.
351	
352	3. **Project-scoped plugins**: Plugins install to the project, not globally. This lets different projects use different plugin versions and makes the project self-contained.
353	
354	4. **Minimal install.sh**: The curl script does one thing (install binary). Everything else is handled by `ddx` commands that have proper error handling, embedded assets, and testability.
355	
356	## Out of Scope
357	
358	- Windows-specific installation (future)
359	- System package manager integration (apt, brew, etc.) (future)
360	- Plugin publishing to registry (future)
361	- Global plugin installation (future — currently project-scoped only)
362	
363	## Acceptance Criteria
364	
365	### AC-001: Clean Machine Installation
366	```bash
367	# In Docker container with nothing installed
368	curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/main/install.sh | bash
369	which ddx            # → ~/.local/bin/ddx
370	ddx version          # → shows version
371	ls ~/.ddx/ 2>&1      # → no such directory (install.sh doesn't create it)
372	```
373	
374	### AC-002: Global Skill Installation
375	Post-consolidation (FEAT-011, 2026-04-17): the embedded tree is the
376	single portable `ddx` skill. Adjust the example from the pre-consolidation
377	`ddx-bead`/`ddx-agent`/etc. listing to match the current layout:
378	
379	```bash
380	# After AC-001
381	ddx install --global
382	ls ~/.ddx/skills/ddx/           # → skill files exist (SKILL.md, reference/*.md)
383	readlink ~/.agents/skills/ddx   # → ../../.ddx/skills/ddx
384	readlink ~/.claude/skills/ddx   # → ../../.agents/skills/ddx
385	```
386	
387	Implementation: `cli/cmd/install_global.go`. Covered by
388	`TestInstallGlobalExtractsSkillsAndChainsSymlinks` and the adjacent
389	idempotency / safety tests.
390	
391	### AC-003: Repository Initialization
392	```bash
393	# In empty project directory
394	ddx init
395	ls .ddx/skills/ddx-doctor/   # → real files (not symlinks)
396	ls .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/ # → real files (copied, not symlinked)
397	ls .claude/skills/ddx-doctor/ # → real files or relative symlink to .agents
398	git add .agents/ .claude/     # → works (real files tracked by git)
399	```
400	
401	### AC-004: Plugin Installation (Project-Scoped)
402	```bash
403	# In initialized project
404	ddx install helix
405	ls .ddx/plugins/helix/                        # → plugin cloned
406	readlink .agents/skills/helix-align           # → ../.ddx/plugins/helix/.agents/skills/helix-align
407	readlink .claude/skills/helix-align           # → ../.agents/skills/helix-align
408	```
409	
410	### AC-005: Missing DDx Detection
411	```bash
412	# Clone a project with .ddx/skills/ddx-doctor/ but no ddx binary
413	# ddx-doctor skill detects missing binary and prompts install
414	```
415	
416	### AC-006: Version Tracking
417	```bash
418	# ddx init writes versions.yaml
419	ddx init
420	cat .ddx/versions.yaml  # → ddx_version: "0.3.0" (current binary version)
421	git log --oneline -1     # → commit includes .ddx/versions.yaml
422	```
423	
424	### AC-007: Version Gate (binary too old)
425	```bash
426	# Simulate: edit versions.yaml to require newer version
427	echo 'ddx_version: "99.0.0"' > .ddx/versions.yaml
428	ddx bead list            # → Error: This project requires DDx v99.0.0 or newer...
429	ddx version              # → works (exempt command)
430	ddx upgrade              # → works (exempt command)
431	```
432	
433	### AC-008: Staleness Hint (binary newer)
434	```bash
435	# Simulate: edit versions.yaml to older version
436	echo 'ddx_version: "0.0.1"' > .ddx/versions.yaml
437	ddx bead list            # → normal output + hint: "💡 Project skills from DDx v0.0.1..."
438	```
439	
440	### AC-009: Force Refresh
441	```bash
442	# After staleness hint
443	ddx init --force
444	cat .ddx/versions.yaml   # → ddx_version updated to current
445	cat .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/SKILL.md  # → overwritten with latest
446	```
447	
448	### AC-010: Dev Build Bypass
449	```bash
450	# Dev build (version="dev") should not trigger gate
451	# Even if versions.yaml says v99.0.0
452	ddx bead list            # → works normally, no error
453	```
454	
455	### AC-011: Docker Test Coverage
456	All above scenarios run in Docker containers:
457	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.clean` — minimal image for AC-001
458	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.with-ddx` — pre-installed for AC-002, AC-003, AC-004
459	- `tests/docker/Dockerfile.no-binary` — ddx removed for AC-005
460	
461	### AC-012: Bootstrap Skill Cleanup on `ddx init`
462	```bash
463	# Simulate a stale bootstrap skill from an older DDx version
464	mkdir -p .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill
465	echo "---" > .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill/SKILL.md
466	ddx init
467	ls .agents/skills/ddx-old-skill 2>&1  # → no such file or directory (removed)
468	ls .agents/skills/ddx-doctor/         # → present (in bootstrap allowlist)
469	ls .agents/skills/ddx-run/            # → present (in bootstrap allowlist)
470	# Plugin skills are untouched

## Summary: Install/Skills Code Architecture

### Key Files Located

#### CLI Commands (`cli/cmd/`)
- **`install.go`** (20 KB) - Main install/uninstall/verify/search/outdated commands
  - `newInstallCommand()` - Package/resource install CLI handler
  - `runInstall()` - Main install logic; handles registry and local installs
  - `installLocal()` - Local plugin installation from directory
  - `commitPluginChanges()` - Auto-commits skill symlinks to git
  - `removeStaleFilesFromInstall()` - Cleanup old file versions
  
- **`skills.go`** (2.2 KB) - Skill validation commands
  - `newSkillsCommand()` - Root skills command
  - `newSkillsCheckCommand()` - Validates SKILL.md files
  - `defaultSkillCheckPaths()` - Default search paths for skills

#### Internal Registry (`cli/internal/registry/`)
- **`installer.go`** (17 KB) - Core installation orchestration
  - `InstallPackage()` - Downloads & installs packages from GitHub releases
  - `InstallResource()` - Installs single resources from ddx-library (persona, etc.)
  - `recordInstalledSkills()` - Tracks skill installations
  - Functions handle symlinks, execute bits, scripts mapping

- **`state.go`** (3.5 KB) - Global installation state tracking
  - **CRITICAL: `installedStatePath()`** - Returns `~/.ddx/installed.yaml`
  - `LoadState()` / `SaveState()` - Read/write YAML state file
  - `ExpandHome()` - Expands `~` prefix for global paths

#### Internal Skills (`cli/internal/skills/`)
- **`install.go`** (7.6 KB) - Cross-platform skill file copy
  - `Install()` - Main entry point; copies skills to `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/`
  - `discoverSkills()` - Finds skills in both plugin layout (`.agents/skills/<name>`) and top-level layout
  - `validateSkillName()` - Security checks (no path traversal)
  - `copyFromFS()` - Recursive copy; explicitly skips symlinks for cross-platform invariant (FEAT-015)

- **`validate.go`** (4.0 KB) - SKILL.md metadata validation
  - `ValidateContent()` - Enforces required frontmatter (name, description)
  - `ValidatePaths()` - Recursively validates SKILL.md files
  - Rejects nested `skill:` frontmatter

### Directory Structure

**Project-local installations:**
```
.ddx/plugins/<name>/          # Package root installation
.agents/skills/<name>/        # Symlinked or copied skill destinations
.claude/skills/<name>/        # Copied skill destinations
```

**Global installation state:**
```
~/.ddx/installed.yaml         # Central registry of all installed packages
```

### Key Functions & Global Paths

| Reference | Path | Location | Notes |
|-----------|------|----------|-------|
| Installation state | `~/.ddx/installed.yaml` | `state.go:28-34` | Single source of truth; YAML format |
| Skill discovery | `.agents/skills/` | `install.go:180` | Auto-committed to git |
| Skill discovery | `.claude/skills/` | `install.go:181` | Auto-committed to git |
| Project skills | `skills/` | `skills.go:70` | Default check path |
| CLI internal skills | `cli/internal/skills` | `skills.go:73` | DDx bootstrap skills |
| Default root target | `.ddx/plugins/<name>/` | (line 77 in installer.go) | Package structure |
| ExpandHome() | Handles `~` prefix | `installer.go:531-541` | Used by all paths |

### Key Design Patterns

1. **FEAT-015 (Cross-platform Invariant)**: Skills are always copied as **real files**, never symlinked. This prevents symlink-related failures on Windows.

2. **Global vs. Project-Local**: 
   - Installation state tracked globally (`~/.ddx/installed.yaml`)
   - Plugin content installed project-locally (`.ddx/plugins/`)
   - Skills copied to both `.agents/skills/` AND `.claude/skills/` (dual-path delivery)

3. **Security Validation**: 
   - `assertWithinRoot()` prevents path traversal attacks
   - Skill names validated against `..`, `/`, `\` separators
   - Symlink resolution checked to prevent escapes

4. **Skill Discovery**: Two source layouts supported:
   - Plugin layout: `.agents/skills/<skillName>` (with fallback to `skills/<skillName>`)
   - Bootstrap/top-level: Direct skill directories

5. **Git Integration**: `commitPluginChanges()` auto-commits:
   - `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`
   - `.agents/skills/`
   - `.claude/skills/`

### No Home-Rooted Plugin Installs

The code explicitly **rejects** any manifest declaring `Root.Target` starting with `~`. Lines 32-34 and 84-86 in `installer.go` enforce this constraint. This prevents plugins from polluting global state.

# Feature: Online Library & Plugin Registry

**ID:** FEAT-009
**Status:** Complete

**Status:** Complete
**Priority:** P0
**Owner:** DDx Team

## Overview

DDx needs to discover, install, and manage resources from an online library. The library is a git repository (`ddx-library`) containing personas, prompts, templates, patterns, MCP configurations, and **package descriptors** for workflow tools like HELIX. DDx fetches resources on demand — no git subtree, no full repo clone.

This replaces the current git-subtree-based sync model with a lighter, more practical approach: DDx downloads what you ask for, caches it locally, and keeps track of what's installed.

## Problem Statement

**Current situation:**
- `ddx-library` exists as a git repo but DDx's sync mechanism (`ddx update`) is a stub
- Git subtree is complex, fragile, and overkill for distributing personas and templates
- HELIX publishes skills independently (`~/.agents/skills/`) with no DDx integration
- Dun expects plugins at `~/.cache/ddx/library/plugins/` but nothing populates this path
- There's no way to discover what's available or install a specific resource

**Desired outcome:** `ddx install helix` fetches and installs HELIX skills. `ddx install persona/strict-code-reviewer` fetches one persona. `ddx search testing` finds testing-related resources. Simple, practical, no git subtree.

# ADR-003: Package Integrity and Supply Chain Security

**Status:** Accepted
**Date:** 2026-04-04
**Context:** DDx installs packages (workflows, personas, plugins) from git-based registries. Downloaded content must be verified to prevent supply chain attacks.

## Decision

Use **commit SHA pinning + content tree hashing** in a version-controlled lockfile. No signing infrastructure. The lockfile is the trust anchor — it's "signed" by whoever committed it via git's own commit authorship.
